title: Class 1 bounded assistance baseline scenario
description: Deterministic scenario skeleton for a representative Class 1 bounded low-risk assistance path. This file is an integration asset and must remain aligned with frozen assets under common/. Class 1 may also be reached after Class 2 clarification when the user or caregiver confirms a bounded low-risk assistance candidate and the Deterministic Validator approves exactly one admissible low-risk action.

steps.description: Publish a representative Class 1 candidate payload through the registry-aligned operational context input plane.
Observe the verification-safe audit stream and capture the routing outcome.

class2_transition_reference.required_condition: User or caregiver confirms a bounded low-risk assistance candidate, the candidate remains inside common/policies/low_risk_actions.json, and the Deterministic Validator approves exactly one admissible action before dispatch.
class2_transition_reference.prohibited_shortcut: Class 2 candidate text or LLM guidance must not directly authorize Class 1 actuation.

notes: This scenario is intended to represent a bounded low-risk assistance path rather than an emergency or direct escalation path.
Exact admissibility assumptions remain defined by frozen policy and schema assets.
Class 1 autonomous execution must remain limited to the frozen low-risk lighting catalog.
Doorlock-sensitive behavior must not be represented as Class 1 autonomous execution.
Class 1 may be reached after Class 2 clarification only after user/caregiver confirmation and deterministic validation against the low-risk catalog.
Class 2 candidate text is not validator output and must not be treated as actuation authority.
Schema-governed context fixtures referenced by this scenario should include environmental_context.doorbell_detected.
